extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNISSEN Mike defended yellow as Jumbo-Visma won the team time trial by 20 seconds over INEOS, who started first and set the benchmark at 29:18.
tactical_patterns: INEOS starting first as defending champions meant they rode blind, allowing Jumbo-Visma to chase a known target throughout.
Deceuninck-QuickStep lost by 0.75 seconds despite closing 2-second gap in final 7km, suggesting pacing error or team depth issues.
THOMAS Geraint crossed first for INEOS, positioning himself for yellow if they won—deliberate leader succession planning despite defending champion status.
Jumbo-Visma kept five riders together while TEUNISSEN considered skipping turns in finale, revealing they prioritized jersey defense over raw time.

rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Mike: positive: considered tactically skipping pulls in finale to conserve energy for jersey defense, showing mature race intelligence beyond surprise sprint win
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: positioned to take first crossing for yellow despite being defending champion, suggesting INEOS already planning leadership transition or flexibility
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: visibly struggling to hold wheels in final kilometer for Jumbo-Visma, potentially limiting their maximum speed in closing section
